history.qwen_observation: Spring Boot 2.7 upgrade fails compilation due to reactor.retry.Retry type mismatch caused by Reactor 3.3+ package relocation.
history.qwen_rationale: Skipping the intermediate Spring Boot 2.7 step avoids the Reactor import incompatibility that cannot be fixed with the allowed recipe set. Jumping directly to the Spring Boot 3.3 meta-recipe under JDK 17 handles the full framework transition, including internal javax-to-jakarta migration and modern Reactor compatibility, aligning with the recommended SB-3 path for 2.x to 21 migrations.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed after Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade due to Reactor 3.4 API changes (removed doAfterSuccessOrError and reactor.retry package rename) not fully resolved by the meta-recipe.
history.qwen_rationale: Following the recommended SB2-to-SB3 migration path, we replace the upgrade_build_to_java17 step with UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 at JDK 17. This meta-recipe handles the framework transition, dependency alignment, and build configuration. We proceed with the standard Java 17/21 transform steps, relying on the SB3 recipe to manage Reactor compatibility and subsequent steps to apply remaining language-level migrations.

history.qwen_observation: Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade fails due to Reactor API removals (doAfterSuccessOrError) and package moves (reactor.retry -> reactor.util.retry) that lack corresponding OpenRewrite migration recipes.
history.qwen_rationale: Skip the problematic Spring Boot meta-recipe and follow the standard Java migration path. Insert a POM_PATCH step to force reactor-core to 3.3.19.RELEASE, which retains the deprecated reactor.retry.Retry class and doAfterSuccessOrError method, allowing compilation to proceed under JDK 17/21 without source-level Reactor refactoring.

history.qwen_observation: Tests fail with Unsupported class file major version 65 because Spring Boot 2.x's bundled ASM cannot parse Java 21 bytecode.
history.qwen_rationale: Replacing the Java 17 build upgrade with the Spring Boot 3.3 meta-recipe upgrades the framework and its dependencies (including ASM) to versions compatible with Java 17+ bytecode, resolving the class file version mismatch during test execution.
